Guide for considering influenza testing when influenza viruses are circulating in the community 
Dec. 19, 2025 
What to know 
This page provides a guide for clinicians considering influenza testing of patients when influenza viruses are circulating in the community, regardless of the patient’s influenza vaccination history. Confirmation of influenza virus infection by diagnostic testing is not required for decisions to prescribe antiviral medication. Decision-making should be based upon signs and symptoms consistent with influenza illness and epidemiologic factors.

Guide for considering influenza testing when flu viruses are circulating in the community (regardless of influenza vaccination history)1 
Confirmation of influenza virus infection by diagnostic testing is not required for decisions to prescribe antiviral medication, although influenza testing can confirm a clinical diagnosis of influenza. Decision-making should be based on signs and symptoms consistent with influenza illness and epidemiologic factors.
